Para Review - Empires & Puzzles 3-Star Dark Healer

Is this rat just pest control or a vital healer for your 3-star team? We dive into Para's kit, comparing this Kalevala healer against modern 3-star titans. Discover if the 'Household Familiar' deserves a spot in your roster or if he's better left in the mouse trap.
Para: The Purple Rat Medic of Kalevala
Para: The Rodent Medic of Kalevala
Para is a 3-star Rare Dark hero from the
event. Released back in the summer of 2022, this peculiar "Household Familiar" (a polite way of saying "giant magical rat with a butter churn") fills a very specific and often under-served niche: the

While he doesn't bring the flashy damage of modern Construct or Glass heroes, his combination of healing and attack reduction makes him a sturdy support option. If you've been playing for a while, you know that finding a reliable healer in the Dark element for 3-star tournaments can sometimes feel like trying to find a piece of cheese in a maze... blindfolded.

Let's address the elephant—or rather, the rodent—in the room. Para is a bipedal rat wearing a cowl, carrying what looks like an oversized wooden butter churn on his back. It's weird, it's rustic, and it fits the Kalevala folklore theme perfectly. The art style is distinct, leaning into the slightly gritty, mythological aesthetic of the event. Compared to the shiny, polished armor of the "Glass" costumes or the high-tech "Constructs," Para looks like he just crawled out of a storybook (and possibly your pantry).
Let's be real: Para's base stats are showing their age. With a Limit Broken (LB2) power of
, he lags significantly behind modern monsters like
(727 Power). His attack and defense stats are modest by 2026 standards. However, his
(at LB2) is surprisingly robust, keeping him competitive in terms of survivability. He won't tank hits like a modern Paladin, but he has enough meat on his bones to fire his special at least once.
"Familiar Witchcraft" is a bread-and-butter support skill.
This is a solid chunk of health for a 3-star hero. It's not a flashy "boosted health" mechanic, but it gets the job done.
This is the unsung hero of his kit. Lowering enemy attack by nearly a quarter significantly increases your team's survivability, effectively acting as a "virtual" defense buff.
Against Holy (Yellow) enemies, the attack down becomes
. This is huge in 3-star tournaments where cleansers are rare and Holy hitters (like Pixie or Poppy) are common.
32% of Max HP. For a teammate with 1200 HP, that's roughly
. It scales well with the high HP pools of Limit Broken 3-stars.
The -24% attack debuff means an incoming hit that would normally deal 500 damage deals roughly 380 damage instead. Over 6 turns, this damage mitigation adds up to hundreds of saved HP.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
Reducing damage from Holy special skills is a nice bonus, especially since you'll often bring Para against Yellow tanks or flanks. It stacks with his innate resistance to being one-shot.
This is the star of the show.
The "Crisis Boost" gives Para 15% HP and

the first time he drops to low health. This can be a lifesaver, often pushing his mana bar from "almost there" to "charged" right when you need a miracle heal. It acts as a safety net that many older heroes lack.

is a practical, if boring, Aether Power. It keeps him alive longer to cast his heal. It doesn't have the game-changing impact of "Regen" or "Special Boost," but for a support hero, staying alive is job number one.
Upon release, Para was a breath of fresh air. Dark 3-star healers were practically non-existent outside of
, and Para offered a pure healing alternative.

The stat creep is real. Modern 3-stars hit harder and have more complex passives. Para's 32% heal is standard, but he lacks the secondary effects (like cleanse or dispell) that define top-tier supports today.
. He is no longer a "must-have" for every team, but in
tournaments or events where you are restricted to Purple, he is an excellent safety valve. He shines when you need to outlast the opponent rather than nuke them.
As of this review, Para does not have a costume. He is relying entirely on his base kit and the kindness of his Kalevala family bonus.

Don't pair with heroes that overwrite attack debuffs with weaker ones (though rare in 3-star).
. Having a healer for your Dark stack is invaluable for completing Rare quests and early map stages. He is cheap to level and will serve you well until you build a deep 4-star roster.
Para is a "depth" hero. You likely won't use him in regular raids, but for
, he is a solid backup if you don't have multiple Treevils or the latest OP event heroes. Don't prioritize Limit Breaking him unless you are serious about competing in 3-star tournaments.
When Para was released, players were relieved to have another Purple healing option.
- A relieved player on the forums.
"Consider Aderyn is thus far the only other 3-star Purple healer... Para is quite a catch."
The community correctly identified that the "Low Health" passive is essentially the Season 3 (Valhalla) passive but slightly better because it gives mana
Some confusion existed initially about his skill text, but it was clarified that the "Uncleansable" part applies specifically against Holy enemies.

Para isn't going to break the game or rewrite the meta. He is a humble, hardworking rat with a bucket. But in the specific ecosystem of 3-star tournaments, he fills a necessary role with competence. He keeps your team alive, makes the enemy hit like wet noodles, and occasionally clutches a win with his passive mana boost.
A solid B-tier hero that jumps to A-tier in Rush Attack. Keep one, but maybe don't invite him to dinner—he brings his own rats.
A comprehensive review and strategy guide for Para, the 3-star Dark healer from the Kalevala family in Empires & Puzzles. Analysis of stats, skills, and usage in tournaments.
